nodes.label: brain
nodes.description: The brain is the center of the nervous system in all vertebrate, and most invertebrate, animals. Some primitive animals such as jellyfish and starfish have a decentralized nervous system without a brain, while sponges lack any nervous system at all. In vertebrates, the brain is located in the head, protected by the skull and close to the primary sensory apparatus of vision, hearing, balance, taste, and smell[WP].

nodes.label: Brain parenchymal tuberculoma
nodes.description: It is estimated that TB in the brain parenchyma develops in nearly one of 300 non-treated cases of pulmonary TB cases, and in half of the patients with disseminated TB. Imaging studies, such as CT and MRI with contrast enhancement, are the basis for diagnosis of tuberculoma. The most common image of tuberculoma is a ring-enhancing lesion due to the absence of blood supply in the caseous necrosis center within the tuberculoma. MRI is slightly superior for showing the size of brain lesions and helps to identify the solid caseous necrosis. Images of caseating tuberculoma are generally composed of three zones, an inner iso-intense and hypo-intense layer image due to caseous necrosis (signals in T1WI and T2WI, respectively). In this zone, fluid-attenuated inversion recovery (FLAIR) images reflect extensive necrosis and hypercellularity. A middle layer with hypo-intense and hyperintense signals due to the presence of Langhans giant cells, epithelioid cells, and edema (in T1WI and T2WI, respectively) is enhanced with gadolinium in contrast images, whereas the external layer shows iso-intense and hypo-intense component (signals in T1WI and T2WI) due to the collagenous capsule.. Tuberculoma results from hematogenous spread of Mycobacterium tuberculosis (M. tb) from an extracranial source. Diagnosis is based on computed tomography (CT) scan and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) studies with a similar ring-enhancing lesion.

nodes.label: brain cancer
nodes.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm affecting the brain.

nodes.label: brain sarcoma
nodes.description: A sarcoma arising from the brain.

nodes.label: nervous system
nodes.description: The nervous system is an organ system containing predominantly neuron and glial cells. In bilaterally symmetrical organism, it is arranged in a network of tree-like structures connected to a central body. The main functions of the nervous system are to regulate and control body functions, and to receive sensory input, process this information, and generate behavior [CUMBO].

nodes.label: Neoplasm of the nervous system
nodes.description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the nervous system.

nodes.label: nervous system process
nodes.description: An organ system process carried out by any of the organs or tissues of the neurological system.

nodes.label: nervous system cancer
nodes.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the nervous system.

nodes.label: nervous system neoplasm
nodes.description: A neoplasm (disease) that involves the nervous system.

nodes.label: substantia nigra
nodes.description: Predominantly gray matter midbrain structure lying dorsal to the crus cerebri and ventral to the midbrain tegmentum. It is divided into a dorsal, cellularly compact region known as the pars compacta and a more ventrally located, containing more loosely packed cells, the pars reticulata. The most lateral region of the reticulata is identified as the pars lateralis (MM).

nodes.label: Substantia nigra gliosis
nodes.description: Focal proliferation of glial cells in the substantia nigra.

nodes.label: neuron of the substantia nigra
nodes.description: A neuron of the substantia nigra.

nodes.label: Focal substantia nigra T2 hyperintensity
nodes.description: Hyperintense lesion in the substantia nigra on magnetic resonance T2 imaging.

nodes.label: collection of basal ganglia
nodes.description: Subcortical masses of gray matter in the forebrain and midbrain that are richly interconnected and so viewed as a functional system. The nuclei usually included are the caudate nucleus (caudoputamen in rodents), putamen, globus pallidus, substantia nigra (pars compacta and pars reticulata) and the subthalamic nucleus. Some also include the nucleus accumbens and ventral pallidum.

nodes.label: Basal ganglia edema
nodes.description: Swelling within the basal ganglia due to the accumulation of fluid.

nodes.label: Basal ganglia calcification
nodes.description: The presence of calcium deposition affecting one or more structures of the basal ganglia.

nodes.label: neuron
nodes.description: These cells are also reportedly CD4-negative and CD200-positive. They are also capable of producing CD40L and IFN-gamma.. The basic cellular unit of nervous tissue. Each neuron consists of a body, an axon, and dendrites. Their purpose is to receive, conduct, and transmit impulses in the nervous system.

nodes.label: Multinucleated neuron
nodes.description: Neurons that contain more than one nucleus.

nodes.label: capillary of brain
nodes.description: A capillary that is part of the brain.

nodes.label: Glomerular capillary collapse
nodes.description: Wrinkling and folding of the glomerular basemement membrane (GBM) with partial or complete occlusion of the capillary lumen: wrinkling and folding of the GBM with partial or complete occlusion of the capillary lumen. The wrinkling is generally made by small and/or big irregular folds of the GBM.. Collapse is generally accompanied by other descriptors such as hypertrophy and hyperplasia of overlying glomerular epithelial cells, halo, protein droplets in podocytes and glomerular parietal epithelial cells (PECs).

nodes.label: Abnormal glomerular capillary morphology
nodes.description: A structural anomaly of the capillary blood vessels in the renal glomerulus.

nodes.label: ependymal tumor of brain
nodes.description: A tumor arising from the ependymal lining of the ventricles.

nodes.label: Insect bite allergy
nodes.description: Hypersensitivity in form of an adverse immune reaction against insect bites.

nodes.label: Abnormal ganglion morphology
nodes.description: An abnormality of nerve cell cluster or a group of nerve cell bodies located in the autonomic nervous system.

nodes.label: Insect allergy
nodes.description: Hypersensitivity in form of an adverse immune reaction against insects.

nodes.label: gasserian ganglion meningioma
nodes.description: A meningioma that affects the trigeminal ganglion.

nodes.label: adult brain ependymoma
nodes.description: An ependymoma of the brain occurring in adults.

nodes.label: Neoplasm of the genitourinary tract
nodes.description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the genitourinary system.

nodes.label: Malignant genitourinary tract tumor
nodes.description: The presence of a malignant neoplasm of the genital system.

nodes.label: biliary tract cancer
nodes.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the biliary tree

nodes.label: subarachnoid space
nodes.description: The space between the arachnoid and pia mater.

nodes.label: Abnormal subarachnoid space morphology
nodes.description: Abnormality in the space in the meninges beneath the arachnoid membrane and above the pia mater that contains the cerebrospinal fluid.

nodes.label: Inferior oblique muscle restriction
nodes.description: In contrast to deficits produces by primary muscle weakness, extraocular muscle restriction cannot move passively any more than actively.. Mechanical limitation of the range of movement of the inferior oblique muscle.

nodes.label: transverse colon cancer
nodes.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the transverse colon.

nodes.label: basal part of pons
nodes.description: Ventral subdivision of the pons which consists of the massive pontine pertuberance on pontine ventral surface.

nodes.label: Abnormal skull base morphology
nodes.description: An abnormality of the base of the skull, which forms the floor of the cranial cavity and separates the brain from other facial structures. The skull base is made up of five bones: the ethmoid, sphenoid, occipital, paired frontal, and paired parietal bones, and is subdivided into 3 regions: the anterior, middle, and posterior cranial fossae. The petro-occipital fissure subdivides the middle cranial fossa into 1 central component and 2 lateral components.

nodes.label: paralytic poliomyelitis
nodes.description: A poliomyelitis that results in destruction located in motor neurons of central nervous system, has material basis in Human poliovirus 1, has material basis in Human poliovirus 2, or has material basis in Human poliovirus 3, which are transmitted by ingestion of food or water contaminated with feces, or transmitted by direct contact with the oral secretions. The infection has symptom loss of reflexes, has symptom muscle spasms, and has symptom acute flaccid paralysis.

nodes.label: anterior dislocation of lens
nodes.tissue_term: principal anterior division of supraoptic nucleus

nodes.label: premature centromere division
nodes.tissue_term: principal anterior division of supraoptic nucleus

nodes.label: Pain at the medial aspect of the elbow
nodes.description: Pain localized to the medial aspect of the elbow.. The medial aspect of the elbow refers to the inner side of the elbow. Conditions characterized by this finding include medial epicondylitis.
nodes.tissue_term: rostral portion of the medial accessory olive

nodes.label: Enlarged cisterna magna
nodes.description: Cerebrospinal fluid produced in the fourth ventricle drains into the cisterna magna via the lateral apertures and median aperture.. Increase in size of the cisterna magna, one of three principal openings in the subarachnoid space between the arachnoid and pia mater, located between the cerebellum and the dorsal surface of the medulla oblongata.

nodes.label: Enlarged fetal cisterna magna
nodes.description: The cisterna magna is measured on a transaxial view of the fetal head angled 15 degrees caudal to the canthomeatal line. The anterior/posterior diameter is taken between the inferior/posterior surface of the vermis of the cerebellum to the inner surface of the cranium. An enlarged cisternal magna is defined by an anterior/posterior diameter of 10 mm or more (PMID:16100637).

nodes.label: tubercle of rib
nodes.description: The tubercle of a rib is an eminence on the posterior surface, at the junction of the neck and body of the rib, and nearer the lower than the upper border. It consists of an articular and a non-articular portion. The articular portion, the lower and more medial of the two, presents a small, oval surface for articulation with the end of the transverse process of the lower of the two vertebrae to which the head is connected. The non-articular portion is a rough elevation, and affords attachment to the ligament of the tubercle. The tubercle is much more prominent in the upper than in the lower ribs[WP].
nodes.tissue_term: islands of Calleja of olfactory tubercle

nodes.label: tubercle of calcaneus
nodes.description: A raised projection on the calcaneus.
nodes.tissue_term: islands of Calleja of olfactory tubercle

nodes.label: Hypoplasia of the olfactory bulb
nodes.description: Underdevelopment of the olfactory bulb.
nodes.tissue_term: islands of Calleja of olfactory tubercle

nodes.label: Paramolar tubercle of bolk
nodes.tissue_term: islands of Calleja of olfactory tubercle

nodes.label: hippocampal formation
nodes.description: Hippocampus (proper) plus dentate gyrus and subiculum[definition derived from NIF comments and ontology alignment].

nodes.label: Anterior polar cataract
nodes.description: A polar cataract that affects the anterior pole of the lens.

nodes.label: flat bone
nodes.description: Examples: cranium, the ilium, sternum, rib cage, the sacrum and the scapula; the occipital, parietal, frontal, nasal, lacrimal, vomer, scapula, os coxC&, sternum, and ribs. A bone that is shaped as a broad flat plate and composed of two thin layers of compact tissue enclosing between them a variable quantity of cancellous tissue, which is the location of red bone marrow.

nodes.label: Craniocervical junction constriction
nodes.description: Congenital or acquired abnormalities of the occipital bone, foramen magnum, or first two cervical vertebrae that decrease the space for the lower brain stem and cervical cord. These malformations can lead to cranial nerve compression, vertebral artery compression, and obstructive hydrocephalus.

nodes.label: rectosigmoid junction neoplasm
nodes.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the rectosigmoid region. Representative examples of benign neoplasms include lipoma and leiomyoma. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma. Rectosigmoid adenomas always exhibit epithelial dysplasia and are considered premalignant neoplasms.

nodes.label: rectosigmoid junction cancer
nodes.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the rectosigmoid area. Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

nodes.label: Decreased activity of mitochondrial ATP synthase complex
nodes.description: A reduction in the activity of the mitochondrial proton-transporting ATP synthase complex, which makes ATP via oxidative phosphorylation, and is sometimes described as Complex V of the electron transport chain.

nodes.label: Carney complex
nodes.description: Carney complex (CNC) is characterized by spotty skin pigmentation, endocrine overactivity and myxomas.

nodes.label: Fractured acetabular part of hip bone
nodes.description: A partial or complete breakage of the acetabular part of hip bone.

nodes.label: Abnormality of the proximal tibial epiphysis
nodes.description: Any abnormality of the proximal epiphysis of the tibia.

nodes.label: pineal parenchymal tumor of intermediate differentiation
nodes.description: This terms classification was reviewed in the context of the Strategic Refinement project (2023) and was determined to be excluded from the endocrine system disorder (MONDO:0005151) ontology branch (https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9310-0163).. A WHO grade II or III pineal parenchymal neoplasm of intermediate-grade malignancy, affecting all ages. It is composed of diffuse sheets or large lobules of uniform cells with mild to moderate nuclear atypia and low to moderate level mitotic activity. (Adapted from WHO)

nodes.label: intermediate malignant teratoma
nodes.description: An immature teratoma characterized by the presence of an intermediate amount of undifferentiated tissues.

nodes.label: olfactory bulb layer
nodes.description: Missing definition for term UBERON:0004001. Please replace it using linked reference.

nodes.label: Lateral thrust
nodes.description: LT visualized during walking is associated with a greater medial knee load and an increased risk of medial knee osteoarthritis (OA) progression.. Lateral thrust (LT) refers to an abnormal knee joint motion in the early stance phase where the knee joint center moves laterally. Also known as varus thrust, LR is defined as the dynamic worsening or abrupt onset of varus (bow-leg) alignment as the limb accepts weight (stance phase), with a return to less varus and more neutral alignment during lift-off and the non-weight-bearing (swing) phase of gait, varus thrust is a potent mechanical risk factor for medial knee osteoarthritis progression.

nodes.label: solitary cyst of breast
nodes.description: A single, fluid-filled cyst in the breast parenchyma.

nodes.label: solitary plasmacytoma of chest wall
nodes.description: A solitary plasmacytoma that arises from the chest wall.

nodes.label: solitary necrotic nodule of the liver
nodes.description: Solitary necrotic tumor of the liver is a rare nonmalignant hepatic lesion characterized by a mass with a completely necrotic core often partially calcified, surrounded by a dense hyalinized fibrous capsule containing elastin fibers. Patients are usually asymptomatic but some may suffer from intermittent abdominal pain or discomfort.

nodes.label: Pain at the posterior aspect of the elbow
nodes.description: Pain localized to the posterior aspect of the elbow.. The posterior aspect of the elbow refers to the back side of the elbow, which forms the prominent bony tip of the elbow.
nodes.tissue_term: paraventricular nucleus of the hypothalamus magnocellular division - posterior magnocellular part lateral zone

nodes.label: hypothalamic neoplasm
nodes.description: A primary or metastatic neoplasm that affects the hypothalamus.
nodes.tissue_term: paraventricular nucleus of the hypothalamus magnocellular division - posterior magnocellular part lateral zone

nodes.label: regional part of nervous system
nodes.description: Any part or collection of parts of the central or peripheral nervous system. Parts may span both CNS and PNS.
nodes.tissue_term: composite part spanning multiple base regional parts of brain

nodes.label: multiple skull base meningioma
nodes.description: Multiple meningiomas that affect the skull base.
nodes.tissue_term: composite part spanning multiple base regional parts of brain

nodes.label: Posterior predominant thick cortex pachygyria
nodes.description: Pachygyria with cortical thickness above 10 mm with and a posterior predominant severity gradient. The severity gradient is determined based on the gyral width, with gyri typically wider than 5mm over the more severely affected regions. Posterior predominant gradient indicates pachygyria more severe other the occipital lobes but also includes a rare perisylvian-predominant pachygyria and a temporal predominant pachygyria.

nodes.label: adrenal cortex adenoma
nodes.description: A benign neoplasm that can arise from any of the adrenal cortical layers. It can be associated with the overproduction of glucocorticoids (Cushings syndrome), androgenic or estrogenic steroids (adrenogenital syndrome), or mineralocorticoids (Conns syndrome). (Sternberg Diagnostic Surgical Pathology, 3rd ed.)

nodes.label: Transverse terminal upper limb defect
nodes.description: A congenital anomaly that appears as an amputation at different levels of the arm, e.g. humerus, proximal forearm, wrist or finger. The limb is missing the terminal (distal) segment(s), with preservation of all the segment(s) proximal to the missing segment.

nodes.label: superior transverse scapular ligament, calcification of, familial
adult central nervous system teratoma
nodes.description: A mature or immature teratoma affecting the central nervous system and occurring in adults.

nodes.label: adult central nervous system choriocarcinoma
nodes.description: A choriocarcinoma of the central nervous system that occurs in an adult.

nodes.label: adult central nervous system germinoma
nodes.description: A central nervous system germinoma that occurs in an adult.

nodes.label: Abnormal dense tubular system
nodes.description: The dense tubular system (DTS) functions to regulate platelet activation by sequestering or releasing calcium, similar to the sarcotubules of skeletal muscle. In resting platelets, the DTS exists as thin elongated membranes. Within 10 seconds of the addition of thrombin, platelets show a major ultrastructural change in their DTS: from the thin elongated form to a rounded vesicular form.. An anomaly of the intracellular membrane complexes known as the dense tubular system.

nodes.label: Digestive system neoplasm
nodes.description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the digestive system.

nodes.label: respiratory system cancer
nodes.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the respiratory system

nodes.label: lymphatic system cancer
nodes.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the lymphatic part of lymphoid system

nodes.label: immune system cancer
nodes.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the immune system

nodes.label: proliferating neuroepithelium
nodes.description: An epithelium that is undergoing proliferation to provide large numbers of neuronal cells.

nodes.label: neural system
nodes.description: A set of neural structures that subserve a specific function, e.g., visual system.

nodes.label: Open neural tube defect
nodes.description: A type of neural tube defect that is not covered by skin.

nodes.label: melanoma and neural system tumor syndrome
nodes.description: Melanoma and neural system tumor syndrome is an extremely rare tumor association characterized by dual predisposition to melanoma and neural system tumors (typically astrocytoma).

nodes.label: neural glioblastoma
nodes.description: A molecular subtype of glioblastoma characterized by the expression of the neural markers NEFL, GABRA1, SYT1, and SLC12A5.

nodes.label: nervous system cell part layer
nodes.description: Single layer of a laminar structure, identified by different density, arrangement or size of cells and processes arranged in flattened layers or lamina[CUMBO].

nodes.label: central nervous system cell part cluster
nodes.description: A multi cell part structure that is part of a central nervous system.

nodes.label: central nervous system germ cell tumor
nodes.description: A unique group of rare tumors of the central nervous system that affect mainly children and adolescents. Their morphologic and biologic profile corresponds to that of homologous germ cell tumors that arise in the gonads and in other extragonadal sites. Representative examples include: germinoma, embryonal carcinoma, yolk sac tumor, choriocarcinoma, and teratoma.

nodes.label: Tarlov cyst
nodes.description: A cerebrospinal fluid-filled nerve root cyst most often localized in the sacral spine.

nodes.label: perineural angioma
nodes.description: A hemangioma arising from perineural tissues.

nodes.label: perineural cyst
nodes.description: Perineural (or Tarlov) cysts are cerebrospinal fluid-filled nerve root cysts most commonly found at the sacral level of the spine, although they can be found in any section of the spine, which can cause progressively painful radiculopathy.

nodes.label: Ventral hernia
nodes.description: Ventral hernia refers to a condition in which abdominal contents protrude through a weakened portion of the abdominal wall.

nodes.label: nut midline carcinoma
nodes.description: A rare, highly aggressive and lethal carcinoma that affects children and young adults. It arises from midline epithelial structures, most commonly the head, neck, and mediastinum. It is a poorly differentiated carcinoma and is characterized by mutations and rearrangement of the NUT gene. A balanced translocation t(15;19) is present that results in the creation of a fusion gene involving the NUT gene, most commonly BRD4-NUT fusion gene.

nodes.label: photoreceptor array
nodes.description: An array of photoreceptors and any supporting cells found in an eye.

nodes.label: photoreceptor cell
nodes.description: A cell specialized in detecting light stimuli that are involved in visual perception.

nodes.label: eye photoreceptor cell
nodes.description: Any photoreceptor cell that is part of some eye.

nodes.label: Single umbilical artery
nodes.description: The umbilical cord normally has two arteries and one vein.. Single umbilical artery (SUA) is the absence of one of the two umbilical arteries surrounding the fetal bladder and in the fetal umbilical cord.

nodes.label: spinal cord lymphoma
nodes.description: A non-Hodgkin or Hodgkin lymphoma that arises in the spinal cord as a primary lesion.

nodes.label: subarachnoid hemorrhage
nodes.description: A serious neurological disorder characterized by intracranial hemorrhage into the subarachnoid space.

nodes.label: acquired aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage
nodes.description: A serious, life threatening rare neurologic disease characterized by a sudden rupture of an intracranial aneurysm into the subarachnoid space. It usually presents with a sudden, severe, excruciating headache accompanied by nausea, vomiting and syncope. Other features may include focal neurological signs, third and sixth nerve palsies, seizures and cardiac failure. Early complications include rebleeding, hydrocephalus, and seizures.

nodes.label: Muscle vacuoles
nodes.description: Presence of abnormal vacuoles (membrane-bound organelles) in the sarcolemma. The vacuoles are often associated with cytoplasmic and occasionally intranuclear eosinophilic inclusions.

nodes.label: Sarcomatoid carcinoma of the tongue
nodes.description: Sarcomatoid (spindle cell) carcinomas of the tongue is a variant of squamous carcinoma of tongue that is monoclonal, having evolved from a conventional squamous carcinoma with dedifferentiation associated with sarcomatoid transformation.

nodes.label: spindle cell rhabdomyosarcoma
nodes.description: An uncommon variant of rhabdomyosarcoma characterized by the presence of whorls of spindle cells forming a storiform pattern. In children it usually arises in the paratesticular region. In adults it usually arises from the deep soft tissues in the head and neck.

nodes.label: spindle cell sarcoma
nodes.description: A malignant mesenchymal neoplasm composed of spindle-shaped cells. This is a morphologic term which can be applied to a wide range of sarcomas.

nodes.label: neural tissue
nodes.description: Portion of tissue in the nervous system which consists of neurons and glial cells, and may also contain parts of the vasculature.

nodes.label: mediastinal neural neoplasm
nodes.description: A neurogenic tumor that arises from the mediastinum. Neural tumors are the most common tumors that arise from the posterior mediastinum. Representative examples include Schwannoma, neurofibroma, and neuroblastoma.

nodes.label: segmental subdivision of nervous system
nodes.description: Any segmental subdivision of a nervous system. Includes metameric developmental segments, such as vertebrates neuromeres.

nodes.label: segmental subdivision of hindbrain
nodes.description: An organ subunit that is part of a hindbrain [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: subdivision of skeletal system
nodes.description: Anatomical cluster consisting of the skeletal elements and articular elements that are part of an individual subdivision of the organism.

nodes.label: Anti-ganglionic acetylcholine receptor antibody positivity
nodes.description: Ganglionic AChR antibodies are found in patients with autoimmune autonomic ganglionopathy (AAG). Patients with AAG typically present with rapid onset of severe autonomic failure. Major clinical features include orthostatic hypotension, gastrointestinal dysmotility, anhidrosis, bladder dysfunction, and sicca symptoms. Impaired pupillary light reflex is often seen.. The presence of autoantibodies (immunoglobulins) in the blood circulation that react against acetylcholine receptors of the ganglionic type.

nodes.label: Anti-rickettsial antibody positivity
nodes.description: The presence of antibodies in the blood circulation that react against a component of a rickettsial bacterium. Rickettsiae are gram-negative bacteria with an obligate intracellular life cycle circulating between mammalian hosts and hematophagous arthropod vectors

nodes.label: Erythema migrans
nodes.description: Erythema migrans develops at the site of the deposition of Borrelia burgdorferi 7 to 14 days (range, 1-36 days) following the detachment of certain Ixodes ticks. The most important differential diagnosis is a localized and transient inflammatory reaction to the bite of an arthropod that is not associated with infection and resolves spontaneously within a day or two, and is usually less than 5 cm in size.. An expanding erythematous (red) skin lesion, usually round or oval, by definition at least 5 cm in size (in largest diameter).

nodes.label: arbovirus infection
nodes.description: A viral infection that is transmitted by an arthropod.

nodes.label: Symmetric spinal nerve root neurofibromas
nodes.description: Multiple neurofibromas of the spinal nerve roots with a symmetric distribution.

nodes.label: Dental root fusion
nodes.description: Root fusion may be formed either by the combining of roots because of cementum deposition with time or as a result of the failure of the Hertwig epithelial root sheath to develop or fuse in the furcation area, which is frequently found in maxillary and mandibular premolars and molars. In mandibular molars, root fusion usually presents as a C-shaped root, whereas in maxillary molars fused roots may show various root shapes, such as two or more roots being fused partially or completely.. An anomaly of the roots of a multi-rooted tooth (such as a molar) in which the roots are not physically divided by bone or periodontal ligament space.

nodes.label: nerve root neoplasm
nodes.description: Benign and malignant neoplasms arising from one or more of the cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, or coccygeal nerve roots. The majority of these tumors are benign. Clinical manifestations may include pain, weakness and loss of sensation along the course of the involved nerve root. Large tumors may cause spinal cord compression.

nodes.label: Bursa fluid crystals
nodes.description: Bursa fluid is found inside bursae -small, fluid-filled sacs located around joints, especially at points of friction (e.g., between bone and tendon or skin). This feature can be ascertained by aspiration of the bursa fluid.. The presence of crystals (microscopic solids that form distinct lattices, with definable fixed angles, faces, walls) in the bursa fluid.

nodes.label: Cerebrospinal fluid eosinophilia
nodes.description: Presence of at least 10% eosinophils in the total cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) leukocyte count.

nodes.label: Neuroma
nodes.description: A tumor made up of nerve cells and nerve fibers.

nodes.label: trigeminal nerve neoplasm
nodes.description: Benign and malignant neoplasms which arise from or metastasize to the trigeminal or fifth cranial nerve which provides sensory innervation to the face, oral cavity and sinuses and the muscles of mastication. Clinical features may include facial pain or sensory loss or weakness of jaw closure.

nodes.label: nerve plexus
nodes.description: Anatomical junction where subdivisions of two or more neural trees interconnect with one another to form a network through which nerve fibers of the constituent nerve trees become regrouped; together with other nerve plexuses, nerves and ganglia, it constitutes the peripheral nervous system. Examples: cervical nerve plexus, brachial nerve plexus, sacral nerve plexus[FMA].

nodes.label: spinal nerve plexus
nodes.description: An intermingling of fiber fascicles from adjacent spinal nerves to form a network.

nodes.label: brachial nerve plexus
nodes.description: An arrangement of nerve fibers, running from the spine where it proceeds through the neck then the axilla and into the arm where it innervates skin and muscle. [WP,modified].

nodes.label: nerve plexus neoplasm
nodes.description: A neoplasm (disease) that involves the nerve plexus.

nodes.label: Visceromegaly
nodes.description: Visceromegaly is enlargement of the internal organs in the abdomen, including liver, spleen, stomach, kidneys, or pancreas.. Abnormal increased size of the viscera of the abdomen.

nodes.label: central nervous system
nodes.description: The central nervous system is the core nervous system that serves an integrating and coordinating function. In vertebrates it consists of the neural tube derivatives: the brain and spinal cord. In invertebrates it includes central ganglia plus nerve cord.

nodes.label: central nervous system leukemia
nodes.description: Leukemia infiltrating the central nervous system structures.

nodes.label: central nervous system sarcoma
nodes.description: A sarcoma that arises from the central nervous system.

nodes.label: myelin
nodes.description: A dielectric (electrically insulating) material consisting of protein and fat that forms a layer, the myelin sheath, usually around only the axon of a neuron. Myelin is about 40 % water; the dry mass of myelin is about 70 - 85 % lipids and about 15 - 30 % proteins. Some of the proteins that make up myelin are myelin basic protein (MBP), myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein (MOG), and proteolipid protein (PLP). The primary lipid of myelin is a glycolipid called galactocerebroside (GalC). The intertwining hydrocarbon chains of sphingomyelin serve to strengthen the myelin sheath.

nodes.label: Myelin outfoldings
nodes.description: This finding can be demonstrated by nerve biopsy (e.g., sural nerve biopsy).. The presence of excessive redundant myelin in the peripheral nerve sheath.

nodes.label: neuron projection bundle connecting eye with brain
nodes.description: for the vertebrate-specific structure, see UBERON:0000941 (cranial nerve II). A neuron projection bundle that connects the retina or its analog in the eye with the brain. This includes the vertebrate optic nerve (not truly a nerve) as well as analogous structures such as the Bolwig nerve in Drosophila.

nodes.label: brain blood vessel
nodes.description: A blood vessel that is part of a brain [Automatically generated definition].

nodes.label: ganglion
nodes.description: A biological tissue mass, most commonly a mass of nerve cell bodies.

nodes.label: basal ganglion
nodes.description: An individual member of a collection of basal ganglia. Basal ganglia are subcortical masses of gray matter in the forebrain and midbrain that are richly interconnected and so viewed as a functional system. The nuclei usually included are the caudate nucleus (caudoputamen in rodents), putamen, globus pallidus, substantia nigra (pars compacta and pars reticulata) and the subthalamic nucleus. Some also include the nucleus accumbens and ventral pallidum[NIF,modified].

nodes.label: vagus X ganglion
nodes.description: The group of sensory neuron cell bodies associated with the vagus nerve.

nodes.label: Anti-Treponema pallidum antibody positivity
nodes.description: The presence of antibodies in the blood circulation that react against a component of Treponema pallidum.

nodes.label: Positive treponema pallidum PCR test in the blood circulation
nodes.description: Treponema pallidum is the causative agent of syphilis.. Detection of nucleic acid of treponema pallidum in the blood circulation by a method such a polymerase chain reaction.

nodes.label: cutaneous syphilis
nodes.description: Cutaneous lesions arising from infection with Treponema pallidum. In the primary stage, 18-21 days following infection, one or more chancres appear. If untreated, the subsequent stages of the disease appear as syphilids. These eruptions are superficial, nondestructive, exanthematic, transient, macular roseolas that may later be maculopapular or papular polymorphous or scaly, pustular, pigmented eruptions.(Arnold, Odom, and James, Andrews Diseases of the Skin, 8th ed, p409)

nodes.label: striatum
nodes.description: A region of the forebrain consisting of the caudate nucleus, putamen and fundus striati.[GO].

nodes.label: Abnormal corpus striatum morphology
nodes.description: The striatum (also known as the neostriatum or striate nucleus) contains the caudate nucleus and putamen, which are separated from one another by a white matter tract called the internal capsule. The striatum is part of the basal ganglia and receives input from the cerebral cortex. Essentially all cortical areas (sensory, motor and associational) project to the striatum. The other major input to the striatum comes from the thalamus, particularly the intralaminar thalamic nuclei.. Abnormality of the striatum, which is the largest nucleus of the basal ganglia, comprising the caudate, putamen and ventral striatum, including the nucleus accumbens.

nodes.label: globus pallidus
nodes.description: BTO and MA are inconsistent w.r.t striatum and pallidum being non-overlapping as in ABA. Note that we have pallidum as part_of basal gangion, so we can make the direct link to basal ganglion. ISBN:1588900649 says: ... a derivative of the diencephalon, seperates as a result of growing fibers of theinternal capsule and is finally displaced into telencephalon. only a small medial remnannt remains, the entopeduncular nucleus. The globus pallidus should be regarded as part of the subthalamus. Subcortical nucleus, functionally part of the basal ganglia, which consists of two segments the external (or lateral) and internal (or medial) separated by the medial medullary lamina in primates. In rodents, The globus pallidus lateral is separated from the medial segment by the fibers of the internal capsule/cerebral peduncle.

nodes.label: Globus pallidus calcification
nodes.description: Pathological deposition of calcium salts in the globus pallidus.

nodes.label: corpus striatum
nodes.description: * According to the 1917 version of Grays Anatomy, it is the combination of the lentiform nucleus (also known as the lenticular nucleus) and the caudate nucleus * According to BrainInfo it is a part of the basal ganglia comprising the globus pallidus and striatum. The corpus striatum (striated body) is a compound structure consisting of the caudate nucleus and the lentiform nucleus, which consists of the putamen and the globus pallidus[WP].

nodes.label: corpus callosum oligodendroglioma
nodes.description: A oligodendroglioma that involves the corpus callosum.
